description: DayPage V3 Wave 2 体验细节打磨 - 修复 DashScope 404、统一编译提示、底部 sticky 编译按钮、Fromm 风输入框 + 长按语音（上滑取消 / 左滑转文字）、Archive 过往日期健壮化

userStories.title: DashScope baseURL 修正 + 失败可观测
userStories.description: As a user, I want compile to actually work (not return 404). As a developer, I want failed compile calls to log URL/status/body for instant diagnosis.
acceptanceCriteria: Change default baseURL in CompilationService.swift from `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` to `https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1`
Update CLAUDE.md line 30 to match the new default baseURL
On DashScope call failure, print `[DashScope] URL=... status=... body=<first 500 chars>` in DEBUG builds
Manually trigger one compile with a real DashScope key — daily page is generated successfully
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Default baseURL switched to compatible-mode/v1. DEBUG builds now print [DashScope] URL/status/body on non-200 or missing HTTP response. Manual API verification deferred — requires real DashScope key in env, not available in CI sandbox.

userStories.title: DayDetailView 三态健壮化 + dateString 格式校验
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap any date in Archive without white-screen or crash. Empty dates should show a friendly empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: Refactor DayDetailView: move file existence checks from computed properties to @State + onAppear async load
Implement explicit 4 states: .compiled, .rawOnly, .empty, .error — each with its own view
.empty state shows icon + '这一天还没有记录' + close button
.error state shows error icon + description + close button
Validate dateString in init with regex `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$` — invalid → .error state
DailyPageView and RawMemoView init/onAppear check file existence; on failure fall through to .error
Any file IO failure prints full path + errno
Manually navigate to dateString='2020-01-01' in Simulator — no crash, shows .empty state
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill
userStories.notes: DayDetailView now has explicit LoadState machine (loading/compiled/rawOnly/empty/error) driven by .task async load. dateString validated via strict regex + DateFormatter round-trip. RawMemoView and DailyPageView also guard on file existence and log full path + errno on IO failure. Build passes; app launches without crashing. Automated Simulator click-through on dateString='2020-01-01' deferred — requires verify-daypage MCP skill (not wired) or manual UI navigation; the .empty / .error paths are covered by compile-time state machine and will be locked in by US-009 unit tests.

userStories.title: Settings 增加「测试 API 连接」按钮
userStories.description: As a user, I want to verify my DashScope API key works immediately when configuring it, instead of waiting until the 02:00 nightly compile fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 「测试连接」 button below DashScope key input in Settings
Button sends minimal request to `{baseURL}/chat/completions` with `[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]` and max_tokens=1
Success: green ✓ with '连接成功 · 模型 xxx'
Failure: red ✗ with HTTP status + tailored hint (401/403 → 'API key 无效或权限不足'; 404 → 'URL 不存在，检查 baseURL'; timeout → '网络不可达，检查代理')
While testing: button shows spinner and is disabled
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill
userStories.notes: testDashScope now resolves baseURL the same way CompilationService.callDashScope does (env override → compatible-mode/v1 default), sends POST {baseURL}/chat/completions with max_tokens=1. Results render inline under each row: green ✓ + '连接成功 · 模型 qwen3.5-plus' on 200; red ✗ + 'HTTP N · <hint>' on failure (401/403 → 'API key 无效或权限不足'; 404 → 'URL 不存在，检查 baseURL'; 429/5xx specific hints; network errors map timeout/NotConnected/CannotFindHost via NSURLError codes). Button shows spinner and is disabled while testing. Whisper/OpenWeather use same result machinery. Build passes; manual Simulator verification deferred (requires real keys in env).

userStories.title: 删除三处冗余编译提示 — 统一到 CompilePromptCard
userStories.description: As a user, I do not want 3 simultaneous '正在编译' indicators on screen. One is enough.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete the in-progress BannerCenter call at TodayViewModel.swift:463 (keep success/failure banners)
Delete CompilingBadge usage at TodayView.swift:69-72
If CompilingBadge has no other usage, delete the component file too
Merge CompilePromptCard compiling state into one line: '正在编译 N 条 memo · [progress / spinner]'
Compile success and failure still flash BannerCenter once
Manually trigger compile in Simulator — exactly one '正在编译' indicator on screen
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill
userStories.notes: Removed initial BannerCenter progress call in TodayViewModel.compile() and the dependent slowTask/cancelTask upgrade paths (the 'kind == .progress' guards became dead code once the seed banner was gone). Retry-callback no-op'd — future retry telemetry should route through a @Published field so CompilePromptCard owns the indicator. Deleted CompilingBadge usage in TodayView header AND the unused struct itself. CompilePromptCard.body now branches on isCompiling: compiling state collapses to ONE row 'Text + ProgressView' instead of header+sublabel; the 'disabled' modifier is gone since the button only renders in non-compiling state. Success/offline/missingApiKey/401/parseError/network failure banners untouched. Build passes; manual Simulator verify-daypage check deferred (skill not wired).

userStories.title: 新建 CompileFooterButton — 滚到底部才优雅淡入
userStories.description: As a user, I want the compile entry to live at the bottom and only appear when I scroll near the bottom of the timeline, instead of dominating the timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Features/Today/CompileFooterButton.swift — capsule shape, 1pt outline, semi-transparent background, sparkle icon + '编译今日 · N 条' text
Remove CompilePromptCard from TodayView.swift:158-171 (timeline)
Mount CompileFooterButton above InputBarView
Use ScrollViewReader + GeometryReader to track scroll offset
Visibility rule: `!isDailyPageCompiled && memoCount > 0 && scrollOffset within 200pt of contentHeight`
Spring animation in/out (response 0.35, damping 0.85), duration < 300ms
While compiling: button morphs to progress state ('正在编译 N 条 memo · [progress]'), disabled — same single indicator from US-004
On compile success: button fades out
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill — scroll up/down, button appears/disappears smoothly without flicker
userStories.notes: New Features/Today/CompileFooterButton.swift defines the sticky capsule button + a CompileFooterAnchor helper view + CompileFooterAnchorPreferenceKey that reports the bottom anchor's minY in the ScrollView coordinate space 'todayScroll'. TodayView removed the pre-compile CompilePromptCard from the timeline (DailyPageEntryCard still renders post-compile), named the ScrollView coordinate space, tracks scrollVisibleHeight via GeometryReader and anchorMinY via onPreferenceChange, and mounts CompileFooterButton between the ScrollView and InputBarView. Visibility rule matches PRD: !isDailyPageCompiled && memoCount > 0 && (isCompiling || anchorMinY <= scrollHeight + 200). Spring(response:0.35, dampingFraction:0.85) applied to both isVisible and isCompiling transitions. Compiling state morphs to 'ProgressView + 正在编译 N 条 memo', button disabled. Registered in project.pbxproj (A10000050 / A20000050). Build: xcodebuild -scheme DayPage → BUILD SUCCEEDED. Simulator flicker-check deferred (verify-daypage MCP not wired).

userStories.title: Archive 日历区分有数据 / 无数据日期（半透明 + 全部可点）
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see at a glance which dates have records on the Archive calendar. No-record dates should be semi-transparent but still tappable.
acceptanceCriteria: On ArchiveView appear, async pre-scan vault/raw/ and vault/wiki/daily/ file listings; cache results into @State Sets
Calendar cell visuals: has daily → solid highlight; raw only → dot marker; neither → 50% transparent gray
All cells (including no-data) remain tappable → push DayDetailView
DayDetailView (from US-002) handles state display
Pre-scan does not block UI
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill — three visual states are visible; tapping any date works
userStories.notes: ArchiveView now pre-scans vault/raw/*.md and vault/wiki/daily/*.md via Task.detached on appear; results land in two @State Set<String> (rawDates, dailyDates). calendarCell(dayNum:) derives a CellDataState (.compiled / .rawOnly / .none) from the sets: .compiled → DSColor.primary solid fill + onPrimary text, .rawOnly → surfaceContainerLow fill + onSurface text + bottom-trailing 4pt dot, .none → 50% translucent surfaceContainerLow + 50% translucent text. Today still gets the 2pt primary stroke in all three states. All cells are tappable — removed the showNoRecordAlert path; handleDateTap(dateStr:) now unconditionally pushes DayDetailView which (US-002) already handles .empty / .error / .rawOnly / .compiled. Pre-scan moved behind a fileprivate enum ArchiveVaultScan.listDateFilenames(in:fileManager:) so Task.detached can call it without capturing self. Build: xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build → BUILD SUCCEEDED. Manual Simulator three-state visual verification deferred (verify-daypage MCP skill not wired in this env).

userStories.title: InputBarV2 视觉重设计 + AttachmentMenuPopover（加号面板）
userStories.description: As a user, I want the input bar to look like Fromm: a rounded outlined input + a `+` button on the left that expands attachment options (camera/photos/file/location) in a popover.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Features/Today/InputBarV2.swift coexisting with old InputBarView (feature flag `useInputBarV2 = true` in Settings)
Input bar style: cornerRadius 18, 1pt outline (DSColor.outlineVariant), vertical padding 10pt
Left `+` button: circular 36pt
Right side: microphone button when empty, send button when text exists
Create Features/Today/AttachmentMenuPopover.swift — 4 menu items (📷 拍照 / 🖼 从相册选 / 📁 附件 / 📍 位置)
Tapping `+` opens popover with spring animation; tapping outside dismisses
Each menu item triggers existing TodayViewModel handlers (capturePhoto, pickPhoto, pickFile, addLocation)
Remove the old keyboard accessory toolbar's 5 icons (mic/camera/photo/file/location) when V2 enabled
Settings toggle: 「使用旧版输入框」 falls back to old InputBarView
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill — V2 input bar matches Fromm aesthetic; `+` popover opens smoothly
userStories.notes: New Features/Today/InputBarV2.swift + AttachmentMenuPopover.swift implement the Fromm-style input bar. Main row is [+ 36pt circle] [capsule TextEditor cornerRadius 18 + 1pt DSColor.outlineVariant stroke + vertical padding 10pt container] [right action — mic when empty, send arrow.up in primary-filled circle when text or attachments exist]. `+` toggles AttachmentMenuPopover overlaid bottomLeading with spring(0.28, 0.85) scale+opacity; each of the 4 rows (📷 拍照 / 🖼 从相册选 / 📁 附件 / 📍 位置) closes the menu then calls the existing TodayViewModel handler (startCameraCapture / pickPhoto via .photosPicker / startFilePicker / fetchLocation). Tap-outside dismissal handled via a near-invisible (opacity 0.001) background layer that only mounts while menu is open. No keyboard accessory toolbar on V2 — all attachment entry points live in the popover per PRD. Legacy InputBarView kept intact as fallback. TodayView branches on @AppStorage('useInputBarV2') Bool (default true); SettingsView → 外观 section surfaces a '使用旧版输入框' toggle (inverted binding so the visible label reads as PRD copy). project.pbxproj registers A10000051/A20000051 (InputBarV2) and A10000052/A20000052 (AttachmentMenuPopover) into Today group + Sources build phase. Build: xcodebuild -scheme DayPage … → BUILD SUCCEEDED. Simulator visual check deferred (verify-daypage MCP skill not wired). PressToTalkButton (US-008) will later slot into the `+`-less-mic-button slot.

userStories.title: PressToTalkButton + RecordingOverlayView — 长按录音三态（原位发送 / 上滑取消 / 左滑转文字）
userStories.description: As a user, I want WeChat-style press-to-talk: hold the mic to record, release to send instantly, swipe up to cancel, swipe left to transcribe-only (fill text field, do not send).
acceptanceCriteria: Create Features/Today/PressToTalkButton.swift using DragGesture(minimumDistance: 0) + @GestureState
Press down → start recording immediately + light haptic + RecordingOverlayView slides up from bottom (waveform + timer)
Hold in place → continue recording; real-time waveform from AVAudioRecorder.averagePower(forChannel: 0), update at >= 20Hz (every 50ms)
Release in original position → stop + send immediately + medium haptic; overlay dismisses; no confirmation dialog
Swipe up >= 80pt → 'cancel armed' state: heavy haptic + overlay turns red + '↑ 松手取消'
Swipe left >= 80pt → 'transcribe armed' state: medium haptic + overlay turns blue + '← 松手转文字'
Release in cancel state → discard recording + light haptic + overlay dismisses
Release in transcribe state → call VoiceService.transcribe(audioURL:), fill result into TodayViewModel.draftText, do NOT send + medium haptic + transcribing spinner during call
Create Features/Today/RecordingOverlayView.swift with 3 visual states (recording / cancelArmed / transcribeArmed)
Threshold (80pt) and haptic intensities centralized in DSTokens (or new InputTokens file)
Use .simultaneousGesture or .highPriorityGesture so DragGesture is not stolen by outer ScrollView
Old VoiceRecordingView sheet path remains as fallback (toggleable in Settings)
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage build` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in Simulator using verify-daypage skill — manually run press→release / swipe-up cancel / swipe-left transcribe
userStories.notes: Three new files: DayPage/DesignSystem/InputTokens.swift (enum InputTokens — cancelSwipeThreshold/transcribeSwipeThreshold = 80pt; press/send/cancelArm/transcribeArm/cancelRelease/transcribeRelease haptic styles), Features/Today/PressToTalkButton.swift (40pt mic circle driven by DragGesture(minimumDistance: 0) + @GestureState; first-edge onChanged emits press-down haptic + calls onPressStart; derivePhase() compares -translation.height/width against thresholds — cancel wins over transcribe on ambiguous diagonals so the 'safer' default fires; onEnded reads last translation and dispatches to onReleaseSend/Cancel/Transcribe with phase-appropriate haptic; attached via .highPriorityGesture so outer ScrollView cannot steal it), Features/Today/RecordingOverlayView.swift (4-mode card — .recording / .cancelArmed / .transcribeArmed / .transcribing — colored background + border + 40-bar live waveform from VoiceService.waveformHistory + monospace 20pt timer; transcribing mode shows ProgressView). InputBarV2 now holds @AppStorage('usePressToTalk') Bool (default true), @StateObject VoiceService.shared, @State PressToTalkPhase. When usePressToTalk && no-content, the right action slot renders PressToTalkButton; else legacy tap-mic path opens VoiceRecordingView sheet as before. Press-to-talk handlers: onPressStart → Task { await voiceService.startRecording() }; onReleaseSend → Task { let r = await voiceService.stopAndTranscribe(); onPressToTalkSend(r) }; onReleaseCancel → voiceService.cancelRecording(); onReleaseTranscribe → Task { stopAndTranscribe; if let transcript, onPressToTalkTranscribe(transcript); remove audio file; reset }. RecordingOverlayView mounts at the top of the InputBarV2 VStack when pressToTalkPhase != .idle, so it sits above the divider/text-field row. TodayView wires onPressToTalkSend → addVoiceAttachment + submitCombinedMemo, and onPressToTalkTranscribe → append to draftText with a single-space separator (do NOT submit). SettingsView → 外观 adds 'Use 旧版语音录制' toggle with inverted binding (storage positive, label legacy copy). project.pbxproj registers A10000053/A20000053 (InputTokens), A10000054/A20000054 (RecordingOverlayView), A10000055/A20000055 (PressToTalkButton); InputTokens added to DesignSystem group, overlay+button added to Today group, all three added to Sources build phase. Build: xcodebuild -scheme DayPage ... build → BUILD SUCCEEDED. Simulator press→release / swipe-up / swipe-left manual verification deferred (verify-daypage MCP not wired).

userStories.title: DayDetailView Unit Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit test coverage for DayDetailView's 4 states so future changes do not regress crash behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Create DayPageTests target if it does not exist (use Swift Testing on Xcode 16+ or XCTest fallback)
Test: dateString='2020-01-01' (no files anywhere) → state == .empty
Test: dateString='abc' (invalid format) → state == .error
Test: write mock raw file only → state == .rawOnly
Test: write mock raw + daily files → state == .compiled
Test: corrupted vault path → state == .error, no crash
Tests use a temp directory mocked into VaultInitializer; clean up in tearDown
`xcodebuild -scheme DayPage test` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created DayPageTests target (PBXNativeTarget T70000001, product bundle com.daypage.DayPageTests) with full pbxproj wiring: PBXBuildFile T10000001, PBXFileReference T20000001 (DayDetailViewStateTests.swift) + T30000001 (xctest product), PBXGroup T50000001 (DayPageTests folder), PBXSourcesBuildPhase T90000001, PBXFrameworksBuildPhase T40000001, PBXTargetDependency T75000001 + PBXContainerItemProxy T76000001 on the DayPage app, XCBuildConfigurations TC0000001/TC0000002 (Debug/Release) using TEST_HOST=$(BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR)/DayPage.app/... + BUNDLE_LOADER=$(TEST_HOST) for app-hosted unit tests, plus XCConfigurationList T80000001. DayPage.xcscheme updated to build DayPageTests.xctest for testing and list it under <TestAction><Testables>. VaultInitializer.vaultURL now reads from an internal static testOverrideURL first (production reads Documents as before). DayDetailView exposes a pure static resolver resolveLoadState(dateString:vaultURL:fileManager:) that returns (LoadState, hasRawFile) — the SwiftUI `.task` delegates to this resolver via Task.detached, keeping the state machine directly unit-testable. DayPageTests/DayDetailViewStateTests.swift covers 7 cases: .empty (no files), .error for invalid regex 'abc', .error for calendar-invalid '2020-02-30', .rawOnly (only raw file), .compiled (raw+daily), .compiled (daily-only, hasRaw=false), .error for missing vault path. setUp mounts a fresh temp directory per test into VaultInitializer.testOverrideURL; tearDown clears the override and removes the temp dir. `xcodebuild -scheme DayPage ... test` → TEST SUCCEEDED, 7/7 passing in 0.028s.
